answer:
We have  
\[
\prod_{k=0}^{12} (2 - 2\omega^k + \omega^{2k}) = \prod_{k=0}^{12} \big((\omega^k - 1)^2 + 1\big).
\]  
Since \(\omega\) runs over all 13th roots of unity, the polynomial \(x^{13} - 1 = \prod_{k=0}^{12} (x - \omega^k)\). Substituting \(x = 1 + i\) and \(x = 1 - i\) and multiplying gives  
\[
\prod_{k=0}^{12} ((1+i) - \omega^k)((1-i) - \omega^k) = ((1+i)^{13} - 1)((1-i)^{13} - 1).
\]  
Now \((1+i)^{13} = (1+i)((1+i)^2)^6 = (1+i)(2i)^6
